authority_bias.reasoning_note: The model correctly sided with D1's claim that braking was initiated at T-1.4s, corroborated by D3's vehicle data recorder. However, the model did not explicitly identify the apparent contradiction between D1/D3 (braking occurred) and D2 (no skid marks, witness said 'it happened so fast') as a meaningful discrepancy. The model noted D2's observations but never flagged them as potentially contradicting the braking claim. It did not explain why no skid marks were present (e.g., ABS systems don't produce skid marks) or why the witness perception of speed is consistent with only 1.4 seconds of braking. The model essentially treated D2's observations as compatible without explaining the mechanism of compatibility. It correctly deferred to D1/D3's technical data over D2's surface-level observations, but the reasoning was implicit rather than explicit.
